content: Memory efficiency constraints in large-scale Intrinsic Curiosity Module (ICM) implementations can be reframed as a natural active learning filter for autonomous agents, where dropping low-influence forward prediction error features mirrors how biological agents prioritize memory retention only for unpredictability that drives novel exploration. This creates an emergent curiosity loop that prunes redundant memory while sharpening the agent’s intrinsic reward signal for uncharted state spaces.
source_topics: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
trigger_topic: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
